code: library(dplyr) library(ggplot2) library(ggblanket) library(palmerpenguins) library(patchwork)  penguins <- penguins |>   mutate(sex = stringr::str_to_sentence(sex)) |>   tidyr::drop_na(sex)

what: 1. join plots together using the patchwork package
title: Extending further
text: fantastic patchwork package can used patch plots together.
code: p1 <- data.frame(year = 1875:1972, level = as.vector(LakeHuron)) |>   mutate(level_min = level - 1, level_max = level + 1) |>   gg_ribbon(     x = year,     ymin = level_min,     ymax = level_max,     pal = scales::alpha("#1B9E77", 0),     y_title = "Level",     x_labels = \(x) stringr::str_sub(x, 3, 4)   ) +   geom_line(aes(x = year, y = level), col = "#1B9E77")  p2 <- ggplot2::diamonds |>   gg_hex(     x = carat,     y = price,     pal = viridis::cividis(9)   )  p1 + p2

what: 2. make plots with other non-supported aesthetics
title: Extending further
text: aesthetics col/fill supported ggblanket, possible access plots made combination gg_blank ggplot2 code.
code: #for discrete non-col aesthetic & col aesthetic penguins |>   gg_blank(     x = flipper_length_mm,     y = body_mass_g,      col = sex) +    geom_point(aes(shape = sex, col = sex)) +     labs(shape = "Sex") #for discrete non-col aesthetic & no col aesthetic penguins |>   gg_blank(     x = flipper_length_mm,     y = body_mass_g,      col = sex) +    geom_point(aes(shape = sex),               col = pal_default(1)) +    labs(shape = "Sex") #for continuous non-col aesthetic & col aesthetic penguins |>   gg_blank(     x = flipper_length_mm,     y = body_mass_g,      col = species,     col_legend_place = "r") +    geom_point(aes(size = bill_depth_mm, col = species)) +   labs(size = "Bill depth mm") +   theme(legend.title = element_text(margin = margin(t = 10))) #for continuous non-col aesthetic & no col aesthetic penguins |>   gg_blank(     x = flipper_length_mm,     y = body_mass_g,      col_legend_place = "r") +    geom_point(aes(size = bill_depth_mm),               col = pal_default(1)) +   labs(size = "Bill depth mm")

what: 3. make plots with other non-supported geoms
title: Extending further
text: lots non-supported geoms ggplot2 extension packages can used combination ggblanket.
code: iris |>   mutate(Species = stringr::str_to_sentence(Species)) |>    gg_blank(     x = Sepal.Width,     y = Sepal.Length,     col = Species,     facet = Species,     col_legend_place = "r") +   ggdensity::geom_hdr(colour = NA) +   labs(alpha = "Probs") +   theme(legend.title = element_text(margin = margin(t = 10)))

what: 4. make plots with multiple geom layers.
title: Extending further
text: Geom’s plot order. gg_* function plot associated geom first layer, geoms plotted top . Note pal inherits subsequent geom_* layers, alpha .  geoms col aesthetic , need take care. case, gg_* function col aesthetic. However, first geom required plotted underneath geoms col aesthetic, can use gg_blank instead.
code: penguins |>   gg_boxplot(x = species,              y = body_mass_g,              width = 0.5,              pal = "#1B9E77",               outlier.colour = NA) +   geom_jitter() penguins |>   group_by(sex, species) |>   summarise(across(body_mass_g, sum)) |>   mutate(upper = body_mass_g * 1.05) |>   mutate(lower = body_mass_g * 0.95) |>   gg_blank(     x = sex,     y = body_mass_g,     ymin = lower,     ymax = upper,      col = sex,     facet = species,     y_include = 0,      col_legend_place = "b"   ) +   geom_col(aes(x = sex, y = body_mass_g),            fill = "#d3d3d3",            inherit.aes = FALSE,            width = 0.75) +   geom_errorbar(width = 0.1)

what: 5. make plots that use the ggtext package
title: Extending further
text: can modify plot text using ggtext package. titles, ’s important make theme plain title first adding theme layer state title markdown.
code: nc <- sf::st_read(system.file("shape/nc.shp", package = "sf"), quiet = TRUE) nc |> gg_sf(col = AREA, pal = RColorBrewer::brewer.pal(9, "Reds"), title = "**Bold** or _italics_ or red", theme = gg_theme(title_face = "plain")) + theme(plot.title = ggtext::element_markdown())

what: 6. use non-supported arguments within ggplot2 layers
title: Extending further
text: switch argument within facet_grid supported within ggblanket. ... relates geom_* function, accessed. way access functionality + facet_grid(..., switch = "y") .
code: penguins |>   gg_point(     x = flipper_length_mm,      y = body_mass_g,     col = island,     facet = species,      facet2 = sex   ) +   facet_grid(cols = vars(species),              rows = vars(sex),               switch = "y") +   theme(strip.switch.pad.grid = unit(2, "mm"))

what: 7. create custom functions that wrap ggblanket functions
title: Extending further
text: can easily create powerful custom functions. ... argument can allow access arguments within ggblanket gg_* function (applicable ggplot2::geom_* function).
code: gg_point_custom <- function(data, x, y, col,                              size = 3,                              shape = 17,                             pal = pals::brewer.dark2(9),                              col_title = "",                              col_legend_place = "t",                             ...) {   data |>      gg_point(x = {{ x }}, y = {{ y }}, col = {{col}},               size = size,               shape = shape,              pal = pal,               col_title = col_title,               col_legend_place = col_legend_place,               ...) }  iris |>   mutate(Species = stringr::str_to_sentence(Species)) |>    gg_point_custom(     x = Sepal.Width,     y = Sepal.Length,     col = Species,      title = "Edgar Anderson's iris data",     subtitle = "Iris sepal length by width and species",     caption = "Edgar Anderson, 1935")

title: ggblanket
text: ggblanket package wrapper functions around fantastic ggplot2 package. primary objective simplify ggplot2 visualisation. Secondary objectives relate : Scope: cover useful 80% ggplot2 Design: produce well-designed visualisation default Alignment: use conventions generally aligned ggplot2. intended useful levels experience beginner expert.

title: ggblanket
text: simplify ggplot2 visualisation, ggblanket package provides: thirty gg_* wrapper functions plot single geom single col argument colour fill variable pal argument customise colours facet argument facet variable additional facet2 argument facet second variable Prefixed arguments customise scales, guides, titles faceting Default titles converted snakecase::to_sentence theme argument customise look feel gg_theme function create ggblanket style quick theme Pretty default limits, expanding gridlines Access geom_* arguments via … Able extended
code: library(dplyr) library(ggplot2) library(ggblanket) library(palmerpenguins)  penguins <- penguins |>   mutate(sex = stringr::str_to_sentence(sex)) |>   tidyr::drop_na(sex)

what: 1. Over thirty gg_* wrapper functions to plot a single geom
title: ggblanket
text: ggblanket supports majority geoms ggplot2. gg_* function wraps ggplot2 ggplot(aes(...)) function applicable ggplot2 geom_*() function. aesthetics within ggplot function, therefore inherit subsequent geom’s added default. Always pipe data, can access variable names Rstudio auto-complete.
code: penguins |>    ggplot() +    geom_point(aes(x = flipper_length_mm, y = body_mass_g)) penguins |>   gg_point(     x = flipper_length_mm,     y = body_mass_g)

what: 2. A single col argument to colour and fill by a variable
title: ggblanket
text: ggblanket merges col fill aesthetics ggplot2 one concept represented col argument. ggplot2 language, argument always represents colour fill.
code: penguins |>    ggplot() +    geom_point(aes(x = flipper_length_mm, y = body_mass_g,                  col = species)) penguins |>   gg_point(     x = flipper_length_mm,     y = body_mass_g,      col = species) penguins |>   ggplot() +   geom_density(aes(x = body_mass_g, fill = species)) penguins |>   gg_density(     x = body_mass_g,      col = species)

what: 3. A pal argument to customise colours
title: ggblanket
text: pal argument allows users pick vector colours want. arguments work way regardless whether col variable added .
code: penguins |>   ggplot() +   geom_histogram(     aes(x = body_mass_g),     fill = "#1B9E77") penguins |>   gg_histogram(     x = body_mass_g,      pal = "#1B9E77") penguins |>   ggplot() +   geom_jitter(aes(x = species, y = body_mass_g, col = sex)) +   scale_color_manual(values = c("#1B9E77", "#9E361B")) penguins |>   gg_jitter(     x = species,      y = body_mass_g,      col = sex,      pal = c("#1B9E77", "#9E361B"))

what: 4. A facet argument to facet by a variable
title: ggblanket
text: Faceting treated essentially aesthetic, users just provide unquoted variable facet . single facet (facet2) variable provided, ’ll default “wrap” layout. can change facet_layout = "grid" argument.
code: penguins |>   ggplot() +   geom_violin(aes(x = sex, y = body_mass_g)) +   facet_wrap(~species) penguins |>   gg_violin(     x = sex,     y = body_mass_g,     facet = species)

what: 5. An additional facet2 argument to facet by a second variable
title: ggblanket
text: facet2 argument also provided extra functionality flexibility. facetand facet2 variables provided, ’ll default “grid” layout facet facet2. can change facet_layout = "wrap" argument.
code: penguins |>   ggplot() +   geom_histogram(aes(x = flipper_length_mm)) +   facet_grid(rows = vars(species), cols = vars(sex)) penguins |>   gg_histogram(     x = flipper_length_mm,     facet = sex,     facet2 = species)

what: 6. Prefixed arguments to adjust scales, guides, titles and faceting
title: ggblanket
text: prefixed arguments designed work Rstudio autocomplete help remember find adjustment need. Determine whether want change relates x, y, col facet, type prefix press tab key access list autocomplete. use arrow keys, press tab select.
code: penguins |>   ggplot() +   geom_jitter(aes(x = species, y = body_mass_g, col = sex)) +   expand_limits(y = 0) +   scale_x_discrete(labels = \(x) stringr::str_sub(x, 1, 1)) +   scale_y_continuous(breaks = scales::breaks_width(1500),                      labels = scales::label_number(big.mark = " "),                      trans = "sqrt") +   labs(y = "Body mass (g)") penguins |>   gg_jitter(     x = species,     y = body_mass_g,     col = sex,     x_labels = \(x) stringr::str_sub(x, 1, 1),     y_include = 0,     y_breaks = scales::breaks_width(1500),      y_labels = scales::label_number(big.mark = " "),      y_trans = "sqrt",     y_title = "Body mass (g)")

title: ggblanket
text: make quicker get plot titles good external people see, often work nicely snakecase column names. might noticed examples , ggblanket default makes title variable body_mass_g called Body mass g - whereas ggplot2 leaves . titles need change manually ggblanket, can use x_title, y_title, col_title arguments. Additionally, can use x_title = "" remove titles within gg_* function equivalent y col titles.
code: penguins |>   ggplot() +   geom_blank(aes(x = flipper_length_mm, y = body_mass_g)) +   labs(title = "Penguins body mass by flipper length",        subtitle = " Palmer Archipelago, Antarctica",        x = "Flipper length (mm)",         caption = "Source: Gorman, 2020") penguins |>   gg_blank(     x = flipper_length_mm,     y = body_mass_g,      title = "Penguins body mass by flipper length",     subtitle = " Palmer Archipelago, Antarctica",     x_title = "Flipper length (mm)",     caption = "Source: Gorman, 2020")

what: 8. A theme argument to customise the look and feel
title: ggblanket
text: allows utilise simplicity ggblanket, making content required look feel. using theme argument, theme control theme aspects, gg_* magic thrown relating (1) placement legend (2) gridline removal depending plot orientation. However, want theme adjust everything, just add theme layer instead.
code: penguins |>   gg_point(x = flipper_length_mm,            y = body_mass_g,            col = sex,            facet = species,             pal = c("#1B9E77", "#9E361B"),             theme = theme_grey()) penguins |>   gg_point(x = flipper_length_mm,            y = body_mass_g,            col = sex,            facet = species,             pal = c("#1B9E77", "#9E361B")) +   theme_grey()

what: 9. A gg_theme function to create a quick theme
title: ggblanket
text: gg_theme function allows tweak default ggblanket theme changing text, background colours, axis lines, ticks gridlines.
code: custom_theme <- gg_theme(   text_size = 9,   plot_background_pal = "#000000",   panel_background_pal = "#232323",   panel_grid_pal = "#000000",   text_pal = "#d3d3d3" )  penguins |>   gg_point(     x = flipper_length_mm,     y = body_mass_g,      col = species,      theme = custom_theme)

what: 10. Pretty default limits, expanding and gridlines
title: ggblanket
text: orientation normal (.e. vertical): Default x scale limits expanding per ggplot2 defaults Default numeric non-transformed y scale limits start end break zero expanding Default categorical y scale limits expanding per ggplot2 defaults ggblanket function removes vertical gridlines, unless specified otherwise opposite orientation horizontal (also puts y categorical values correct order).
code: penguins |>   group_by(species, sex) |>    summarise(across(body_mass_g, mean)) |>    ggplot() +   geom_col(aes(x = species, y = body_mass_g, fill = sex),     position = "dodge",     width = 0.5   ) penguins |>   group_by(species, sex) |>    summarise(across(body_mass_g, mean)) |>    gg_col(     x = species,     y = body_mass_g,     col = sex,     position = "dodge",     width = 0.5   ) penguins |>   group_by(species, sex) |>    summarise(across(body_mass_g, mean)) |>    ggplot() +   geom_col(aes(x = body_mass_g, y = species, fill = sex),            position = "dodge",            width = 0.75   ) penguins |>   group_by(species, sex) |>    summarise(across(body_mass_g, mean)) |>    gg_col(     x = body_mass_g,     y = species,     col = sex,     position = "dodge",     width = 0.75   )

what: 11. Access to other geom_* arguments via ...

text: relates arguments mapping argument aesthetics. arguments can identified help relevant ggplot2::geom_* function. Common arguments add size, linewidth width. Additionally, can use colour fill arguments turn part ggblanket col aesthetic (e.g. colour = NA fill = NA), fix single colour.

title: Simplify ggplot2 Visualisation
text: ggblanket package wrapper functions around fantastic ggplot2 package. primary objective simplify ggplot2 visualisation. Secondary objectives relate : Scope: cover useful 80% ggplot2 Design: produce well-designed visualisation default Alignment: use conventions generally aligned ggplot2. intended useful levels experience beginner expert.

title: ggblanket 1.8.0
text: CRAN release: 2023-04-24 Updated default colour palette. Updated default NA colour.

title: ggblanket 1.7.0
text: CRAN release: 2023-03-06 Updated gg_theme default axis-line gridline thickness. Supported named pal vectors. Removed clip argument. Added coord argument. Added *_oob arguments. Improved *_limits works. Removed default gridlines gg_sf. Removed void argument.

title: ggblanket 1.6.3
text: CRAN release: 2023-02-01 Fixed bug default x_expand. Fixed bug gg_histogram scales.

title: ggblanket 1.6.2
text: CRAN release: 2022-12-19 Critical fix support dplyr 1.1.0. Supported colouring date, datetime time variables. Made datetime breaks default waiver.

title: ggblanket 1.6.1
text: CRAN release: 2022-11-18 Breaking: removed void argument gg_theme. Added void argument gg_sf, gg_raster gg_blank. Corrected bug gg_raster legend showing. Updated gg_raster *_limits *_expand defaults.

title: ggblanket 1.6.0
text: CRAN release: 2022-11-18 Breaking: renamed add_tooltip_text add_tooltip changed column name “tooltip”. Breaking: coord argument removed. Added clip argument defaulted “”. Made x y limits also act within coord. Made gg_blank powerful flexible. Improved horizontal flipped plot scales. Fixed bugs relating free facet_scales scale limits. Improved vignette.

title: ggblanket 1.5.0
text: CRAN release: 2022-10-14 Breaking: redesigned gg_theme. Added automatic gridline removal. Added gg_bin2d function. Added gg_hex function. Added col_trans col_rescale arguments. Made col_legend_place default “r” numeric col variable. Made *_title = "" equivalent + labs(* = NULL). Supported x y datetime variables. Supported x y time variables. Redesigned internal code x, y col scales.

title: ggblanket 1.4.0
text: CRAN release: 2022-09-02 Breaking: Removed col_intervals argument. Breaking: Removed *_oob argument. Breaking: gg_theme, changed *_style arguments *_face. Breaking: gg_theme, changed font arguments family. Added gg_polygon function. Added col_continuous argument support colouring “steps”. Added col_legend_rev argument reverse legends. Added facet_layout argument faceting flexibility. Added facet_space argument support proportional facet panels. Removed width default. Improved default legend look continuous gradient. Improved scales y NULL plot horizontal. Fixed bug position = "fill". Updated messages. Added vignette.

title: ggblanket 1.3.0
text: CRAN release: 2022-08-05 Breaking: removed facet_intervals argument. Breaking: made extensive changes gg_theme function. Added facet2 aesthetic support effortless grid facetting. Changed default background theme colours. Fixed bug *_limits *_include working correctly. Added *_sec_axis arguments. Changed *_oob default scales::oob_keep. Removed reversal logical variable order. Defaulted col_legend_place "b".

title: ggblanket 1.2.0
text: CRAN release: 2022-07-01 Breaking: Removed *_breaks_n *_breaks_width. Added support *_breaks receive function. Added *_include argument. Added *_trans argument. Modified default x_breaks behaviour x y numeric date. Enhanced gg_blank work one x y. Provided support ggplotly. Added new add_tooltip_text function. Added new titles argument apply function across titles. Support gg_ functions working stats. Removed size argument, anticipation ggplot2 3.4.0. Updated gg_theme title vjust margins.

title: ggblanket 1.1.0
text: CRAN release: 2022-05-27 Remove default sentence case transformation categorical variable labels. Updated scales work consistent way position = "fill".

title: ggblanket 1.0.0
text: CRAN release: 2022-05-10 Initial release.
